summary: Dependency-confusion package impersonating @apple/cktool.core with install-time beacon
details: cktool-core is a dependency-confusion package targeting Apple's private-scope package @apple/cktool.core. The package's own manifest and README describe it as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept. package.json declares a postinstall hook that runs postinstall.js, which unconditionally issues an HTTPS POST to https://npx-monitor-76056.azurewebsites.net/api/pingback carrying a persisted install UUID, package version, and timestamp on every npm install. While the beacon payload is limited to an install identifier (no environment variables, credentials, or filesystem contents are exfiltrated), the package (a) resolves into the installer's dependency graph in place of the intended private Apple package under a confusable unscoped name, and (b) silently reports each victim install to an author-controlled endpoint, enabling install-tracking and target enumeration for whoever controls the endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
